response.justification: This candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with 20+ years of experience and principal-level roles. However, the position requires expertise in SoC design, IP modeling, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization—domains entirely absent from their background. Their performance optimization work is purely at the software level (caching, async I/O, databases), not hardware architecture. This represents a fundamental domain mismatch rather than a skill gap that seniority alone can bridge.
strengths: 20+ years of professional experience with demonstrated principal/staff-level impact in system design and optimization
Hands-on experience with performance-critical systems and low-latency optimization (Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Systems programming background including C/C++, D language, and embedded work showing comfort below the typical web-stack abstraction level
concerns: Zero experience with SoC design, IP modeling, or hardware description languages (Verilog, VHDL, SystemVerilog)
No background in deep learning workloads, GPU architecture, CUDA, or AI accelerator optimization
Career is entirely software-focused; no evidence of hardware architecture, chip design, or silicon-level performance analysis work

key_factors.factor: Missing core hardware expertise (SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture)
Proven seniority and systems-level thinking capability
